Spec preset for the Wolf 2008 responsive-personalities reproduction

Source: R/scenarios.R
wolf2008_responsiveness_specs.Rd

Returns a specs list configured to test the negative-frequency-dependent- selection mechanism from Wolf, van Doorn & Weissing (2008) PNAS 105:15825-15830, in clade's spatially-explicit framework. See the vignette paper-wolf2008.Rmd for the full reproduction context.

Usage

wolf2008_responsiveness_specs()

Value

A specs list ready for run_alife().

Details

Key parameter choices vs default_specs():

responsive_personalities = TRUE

Enable the module.

grid_rows/grid_cols = 30L

Standard grid; rich-cell competition emerges naturally from clade's grass economy.

n_agents_init = 200L

Density 22% — high enough that responsiveness's frequency-dependent cost actually bites.

max_agents = 800L

Room for population growth; the regulator is grass + handling time, not max_agents.

max_ticks = 3000L

~15 generations at default max_age = 200. Long enough for the responsiveness trait to evolve toward its equilibrium frequency.

ploidy = 1L

Haploid asexual; cleaner trait dynamics.

responsiveness_cost = 0.1

Calibrated below the default_specs() value of 0.4 so populations don't collapse during trait evolution. The frequency-dependent benefit mechanism is preserved either way.

Wolf 2008-specific parameters not listed above (responsiveness_init_mean, mutation SD) inherit from default_specs(). To probe the density-dependent benefit (Wolf's headline mechanism):

for (n_init in c(50L, 100L, 200L, 400L)) {
  s <- wolf2008_responsiveness_specs()
  s$n_agents_init <- n_init
  env <- run_alife(s)
  # ... compute mean responsiveness at end and plot vs n_init
}

See also

default_specs(), run_alife(), wolf_personality_specs().
